ocr.text: As the automotive industry continues to navigate the complexities of environmental regulations and compliance, the
Volkswagen Group remains committed to meeting stringent emission standards while managing associated costs effectively.
The recent analysis of emission compliance costs provides critical insights into the financial implications of these regulatory
requirements over recent years. Notably, the data reveals that the yearly change in compliance costs has reached a significant
figure, with the compliance costs amounting to £500M. This figure underscores the increasing financial burden that
manufacturers face as they strive to enhance their vehicle emissions performance. Furthermore, the report indicates a
percentage change in costs of 1.25%, which reflects the ongoing adjustments necessary to align with evolving regulations and

technological advancements in emissions control. 

In assessing these financial metrics, one can observe that the Volkswagen
Group experienced a yearly change of 5%, illustrating the dynamic nature of the compliance landscape.

This figure is indicative of the broader trends affecting the automotive sector, as manufacturers are compelled to invest
substantially in research and development to create cleaner technologies while also managing operational costs. The
commitment to reducing emissions is not merely a regulatory requirement but is increasingly seen as a corporate responsibility

that aligns with consumer expectations and global sustainability goals.

The complexities surrounding emission compliance costs
necessitate a thorough understanding of both the immediate and long-term financial implications. As the automotive industry
adapts to these challenges, the Volkswagen Group is focused on strategic investments that address compliance while also
fostering innovation in mobility solutions. The data presented highlights the balance that must be struck between regulatory
adherence and financial viability, particularly in light of the notable percentage change of 1.15% observed in the latest fiscal
period. Such metrics are vital for stakeholders aiming to comprehend the financial landscape in which the automotive industry

operates, especially in a time of rapid change and heightened scrutiny over environmental practices.

Through continuous
monitoring and analysis of emission compliance costs, the Volkswagen Group aims to not only comply with regulations but also
to lead the industry in sustainable practices. This proactive approach is essential for maintaining competitive advantage while
contributing positively to global efforts in reducing greenhouse gas emissions. The journey towards compliance is multifaceted,
and the challenges presented by these costs will require innovative solutions and collaboration across the automotive value

chain. 

In conclusion, the emission compliance costs data table serves as a crucial reference point for understanding the financial
pressures impacting the Volkswagen Group and the broader automotive sector. With the yearly change recorded at £500M and a
corresponding percentage change of 1.25%, stakeholders must remain vigilant in their strategic planning and operational
execution to navigate the evolving regulatory landscape successfully. The commitment to reducing emissions through efficient
compliance strategies will ultimately determine the resilience and sustainability of the automotive industry in the years to come.
